title: Pet Activity & Health Monitor Collars: Are They Useful?
meta: Activity monitors for dogs and cats compared on tracking accuracy, battery and health insights.
intro: A health monitor collar tracks sleep, activity and even heart rate — useful for senior pets or weight management. We look at what's signal vs. noise.

sections.body: Most track steps, rest and calories. Higher-end units estimate heart/respiration via optical sensors. Treat the numbers as trends, not veterinary diagnostics.
A monitor your dog hates wearing is useless. Prioritize light weight and multi-day battery so you're not charging nightly.
Most valuable for senior pets (catch activity drops early) and overweight pets (verify exercise). For a healthy young dog, a basic tracker is enough.

faq.a: No. It's an early-warning trend tool, not a diagnostic. Always consult a vet for symptoms.
Activity counts are decent; heart/respiration estimates are rough and vary by fit.
